name: Tick-Borne Encephalitis
description: Tick-borne encephalitis (TBE) is a viral neurological disease transmitted by Ixodes ticks, increasingly reported in dogs across Europe and parts of the UK. It causes severe brain and spinal cord inflammation and has a guarded prognosis.
treatment_overview: There is no specific antiviral treatment. Supportive care with corticosteroids for inflammation, anticonvulsants for seizures, and IV fluids. A dog-specific TBE vaccine is available in some European countries.
prevention_tips: Regular tick prevention with licensed products
Vaccinate if available and in endemic areas
Check for and promptly remove ticks
affected_breeds: all breeds (outdoor dogs in endemic areas)
